codeReview.summary: Implementation satisfies all expected behaviors: deterministic unique multi-query fan-out from modifiers with cap truncation (generateFocusedSubQueries), ordered 3-rung retry ladder (buildRetryLadder: 0=focused, 1=drop location, 2=broaden role/keywords), first-rung success short-circuit (executeQueryWithRetry lines 216-222), and finite exhaustion attribution with GroundedSearchDiagnostics. 11 new tests cover all VAL-ROUTE assertions (012, 013, 014, 017) plus edge cases for disabled flags.

issues.description: Location token matching in buildRetryLadder is word-based. Multi-word locations like 'United States' are split into tokens 'United' and 'States', so rung 1 (drop location) only removes single-word locations like 'Remote'. This may cause multi-word location tokens to persist in rung 1 queries. Consider splitting location strings and filtering each token, or documenting this as a known limitation of the current token-based approach.

executeSingleQuery returns empty candidates without throwing on HTTP errors. This allows the retry ladder to continue, which is arguably correct for resilience. However, the caller executeQueryWithRetry doesn't distinguish between 'zero candidates because query succeeded but found nothing' vs 'zero candidates because query failed'. This could mask HTTP errors that should potentially halt the ladder. If intentional (fail-open for resilience), consider documenting this design decision.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill procedure for UltraPlan features specifies 'deterministic fixtures for fan-out query count/cap and uniqueness' and 'retry rung order, first-rung short-circuit, and exhaustion attribution' in step 6. The worker followed this precisely, generating comprehensive tests covering all VAL-ROUTE assertions plus edge cases. No deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: 11 tests added to grounded-search.test.ts covering VAL-ROUTE-012 (fan-out, cap, determinism), VAL-ROUTE-013 (ladder order, at-most-once), VAL-ROUTE-014 (first-rung short-circuit), VAL-ROUTE-017 (exhaustion attribution), plus disabled-flag edge cases.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No convention gaps observed. The implementation follows existing patterns in grounded-search.ts for client creation, fetch handling, candidate merging, and diagnostics. New types (GroundedQueryEvidence, GroundedSearchDiagnostics) follow the same composable type pattern as existing ExtractionDiagnostic types.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: integrations/browser-use-discovery/src/grounding/grounded-search.ts lines 85-115 define new diagnostics types following established patterns.

summary: Feature implementation passes scrutiny. All four expected behaviors are correctly implemented: (1) deterministic unique multi-query fan-out with cap truncation via generateFocusedSubQueries, (2) ordered retry-broadening ladder (0→1→2) via buildRetryLadder, (3) first-rung success short-circuit in executeQueryWithRetry, and (4) finite exhaustion attribution in GroundedSearchDiagnostics. 11 tests cover all VAL-ROUTE assertions. Two non-blocking observations: (a) location token matching may not handle multi-word locations fully, (b) HTTP errors don't halt the retry ladder (fail-open resilience pattern).
